source.book: 《9分达人雅思听力真题还原及解析6》

segments.text: Woman: Good afternoon, our beloved listeners.
segments.translation: 各位亲爱的听众，下午好。

segments.text: Woman: Welcome to today's Capturing the Moment programme.
segments.translation: 欢迎收听今天的《捕捉瞬间》节目。

segments.text: Woman: Today, we have Darren Almond here with us.
segments.translation: 今天，达伦·阿尔蒙德来到了我们的节目现场。

segments.text: Woman: He's a college student who has just won an award for his set of shots of red squirrels.
segments.translation: 他是一名大学生，刚刚凭借一组红松鼠的照片获了奖。

segments.text: Woman: Welcome, Darren.
segments.translation: 欢迎你，达伦。

segments.text: Woman: Now, can you tell us a bit about this lovely creature and how you managed to take such wonderful photos?
segments.translation: 那么，你能跟我们讲讲这种可爱的小动物，以及你是怎么拍出这么棒的照片的吗？

segments.text: Darren: Well, the red squirrel is Britain's only native squirrel.
segments.translation: 嗯，红松鼠是英国唯一的本土松鼠。

segments.text: Darren: It's graceful and athletic.
segments.translation: 它优雅又灵活。

segments.text: Darren: Its Latin name 'Sciurus Vulgaris' comes from the Greek words, describing a common type of animal which sits in the shadow of its own tail.
segments.translation: 它的拉丁名'Sciurus Vulgaris'来自希腊语，形容一种常见的、坐在自己尾巴阴影下的动物。

segments.text: Darren: At first, I thought this description was funny and was a kind of exaggeration.
segments.translation: 一开始，我觉得这个描述挺好笑的，像是一种夸张。

segments.text: Darren: But now I realize that this name suits them very well.
segments.translation: 但现在我意识到，这个名字非常贴切。

segments.text: Darren: Their fluffy tails under the sun normally cast a big shadow over themselves.
segments.translation: 它们蓬松的尾巴在阳光下往往会在自己身上投下一大片阴影。

segments.text: Darren: You will be lucky to see a red squirrel in the areas where they still survive, due to its timid nature and decreasing number in the wild.
segments.translation: 由于红松鼠生性胆小、野外数量又在减少，你能在它们还幸存的地区看到一只，那可算是运气不错。

segments.text: Darren: In the late 1800s when grey squirrels, an exotic species, were first introduced into Britain, there were only about 20,000 red squirrels left, the majority of which lived in the north of England.
segments.translation: 在19世纪末，当灰松鼠这种外来物种首次被引入英国时，红松鼠只剩下大约两万只，其中大多数生活在英格兰北部。

segments.text: Darren: The greys carry a disease called the squirrelpox virus which could lead to the death of red squirrels.
segments.translation: 灰松鼠携带一种叫松鼠痘病毒的疾病，可能导致红松鼠死亡。

segments.text: Darren: But their number had already declined before the disease spread there.
segments.translation: 但在这种病传播过去之前，它们的数量其实就已经在下降了。

segments.text: Darren: The shrinking population actually corresponds to decreases in the number of nuts available, the red squirrels' primary food source.
segments.translation: 数量的减少其实和坚果供应量的下降相吻合，而坚果正是红松鼠的主要食物来源。

segments.text: Darren: I accidentally left some nuts in my garden one day.
segments.translation: 有一天我不小心把一些坚果落在了自家花园里。

segments.text: Darren: And I found they had been eaten the next morning.
segments.translation: 结果第二天早上我发现它们被吃掉了。

segments.text: Darren: It could have been rats as they have a very similar food category to red squirrels.
segments.translation: 本来可能是老鼠吃的，因为它们和红松鼠的食物种类很相似。

segments.text: Darren: But I knew it was red squirrels because of their different feeding habits.
segments.translation: 但我知道那是红松鼠，因为它们的进食习惯不一样。

segments.text: Darren: Rats bite a hole in the nuts, whereas red squirrels crack the nuts in half and eat the kernels.
segments.translation: 老鼠会在坚果上咬个洞，而红松鼠会把坚果掰成两半，吃里面的果仁。

segments.text: Darren: After a week or so, the red squirrels became regular visitors to my garden, making them ideal subjects for my photography project.
segments.translation: 大概过了一个星期，红松鼠就成了我花园的常客，成了我摄影项目的理想拍摄对象。

segments.text: Darren: Unfortunately, I had to shoot only in my garden, partly because I couldn't find another spot to sight red squirrels on a regular basis.
segments.translation: 可惜，我只能在自家花园里拍，一方面是因为我找不到别的能稳定看到红松鼠的地方。

segments.text: Darren: Also, this could block out any outside distractions, and I could focus solely on my subject.
segments.translation: 另一方面，这样也能屏蔽掉外界的干扰，让我可以专注于拍摄对象。

segments.text: Darren: During the shooting, other animals like birds got caught in the frame together sometimes.
segments.translation: 拍摄过程中，有时鸟之类的其他动物也会一起入镜。

segments.text: Darren: I'm more than satisfied with my photos.
segments.translation: 我对自己的照片非常满意。

segments.text: Darren: Even though I only practiced a few times and used just a second-hand camera from my friend, my great mentor helped me with the shooting.
segments.translation: 尽管我只练习了几次，用的还只是朋友的一台二手相机，但我那位了不起的导师在拍摄上帮了我很多。

segments.text: Darren: I got great tutorials from him and was able to shoot better photos.
segments.translation: 我从他那儿得到了很好的指导，才能拍出更好的照片。

segments.text: Woman: Thank you, Darren.
segments.translation: 谢谢你，达伦。

segments.text: Woman: The award-winning photos will be on display in a local museum for the coming month.
segments.translation: 这些获奖照片将在接下来的一个月在当地一家博物馆展出。

segments.text: Woman: We are looking forward to the exhibition.
segments.translation: 我们很期待这次展览。

segments.text: Woman: So, tell us about the award.
segments.translation: 那，跟我们说说这个奖吧。

segments.text: Darren: OK.
segments.translation: 好的。

segments.text: Darren: I sent my portfolio for the competition hosted by a national newspaper.
segments.translation: 我把我的作品集投给了一家全国性报纸举办的比赛。

segments.text: Darren: I wasn't expecting the third prize.
segments.translation: 我本来没指望能拿三等奖。

segments.text: Darren: So, when they notified me of the result on the phone, I was really excited.
segments.translation: 所以当他们打电话通知我结果时，我真的特别激动。

segments.text: Woman: Do you have some tips for our audience to help improve their photography skills?
segments.translation: 你有什么技巧能分享给我们的听众，帮他们提升摄影水平吗？

segments.text: Darren: Of course.
segments.translation: 当然有。

segments.text: Darren: Here are three things I think photographers can work on to get a better shot.
segments.translation: 这里有三点我觉得摄影师可以下功夫、从而拍出更好照片的地方。

segments.text: Darren: First of all, good composition is key to a lovely shot.
segments.translation: 首先，好的构图是拍出一张好照片的关键。

segments.text: Darren: Normally, an image's centre of interest is placed at one of the intersections that trisect the whole picture.
segments.translation: 通常，一张图片的兴趣中心会放在把整幅画面三等分的交叉点之一上。

segments.text: Darren: In order to achieve balance, a 'Secondary Object', so to speak, can be added to the scene at the opposing intersection.
segments.translation: 为了达到平衡，可以说，可以在画面另一侧的交叉点上加一个'次要物体'。

segments.text: Darren: So, for instance, in one of my shots, a red squirrel at the top left is staring at a nut at the lower right corner.
segments.translation: 比如说，在我的一张照片里，左上角有一只红松鼠，正盯着右下角的一颗坚果。

segments.text: Darren: This is called 'the Rule of Thirds'.
segments.translation: 这就叫'三分法则'。

segments.text: Darren: Lighting is another essential element if you want to be rewarded with a superb photo.
segments.translation: 如果你想拍出一张出色的照片，光线是另一个关键要素。

segments.text: Darren: The direction of the light falling on your subject is most important of all.
segments.translation: 光线照在拍摄对象上的方向是最重要的。

segments.text: Darren: You need to look at your subject carefully and watch how the shadows fall.
segments.translation: 你得仔细观察你的拍摄对象，留意阴影是怎么落下来的。

segments.text: Darren: If you're able to choose the time of day to shoot your pictures, lighting is most ideal in the late afternoon.
segments.translation: 如果你能选择拍摄的时间段，那么傍晚时分的光线最理想。

segments.text: Darren: Try to position yourself so that the sun hits your subject from the side.
segments.translation: 尽量调整自己的位置，让阳光从侧面照到拍摄对象上。

segments.text: Darren: This helps create a 3D effect in the picture.
segments.translation: 这有助于在画面里营造出立体感。

segments.text: Darren: To further improve on your photography skills, it is advised to take detailed notes of timing, position, weather, etc.
segments.translation: 要进一步提升摄影水平，建议你把时间、位置、天气等细节都详细记下来。

segments.text: Darren: By doing so, it is easier to review and adjust accordingly, and there is always room for improvement.
segments.translation: 这样做，回顾和相应调整都会更容易，而且总是有改进的空间。
